# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.

Use safe data-only formats and validate the resulting structure/types before use.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

namespace App\Session;

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Cookie;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Validator;

class UserPreferences
{
    private $preferences = [];
    private $cookieName = 'user_prefs';
    private $cookieLifetime = 43200; // 12 hours

    public function __construct()
    {
        $this->loadPreferences();
    }

    public function set($key, $value)
    {
        $this->preferences[$key] = $value;
        $this->savePreferences();
        return $this;
    }

    public function get($key, $default = null)
    {
        return $this->preferences[$key] ?? $default;
    }

    public function all()
    {
        return $this->preferences;
    }

    public function remove($key)
    {
        unset($this->preferences[$key]);
        $this->savePreferences();
        return $this;
    }

    public function clear()
    {
        $this->preferences = [];
        Cookie::queue(Cookie::forget($this->cookieName));
        return $this;
    }

    private function loadPreferences()
    {
        if (isset($_COOKIE[$this->cookieName])) {
            $data = $_COOKIE[$this->cookieName];
            
            // Validate cookie data format
            if (!is_string($data)) {
                return;
            }

            // Attempt to unserialize
            $unserialized = @unserialize($data, ['allowed_classes' => false]);
            
            // Validate structure
            if (is_array($unserialized)) {
                $this->preferences = $this->validatePreferences($unserialized);
            }
        }
    }

    private function savePreferences()
    {
        $serialized = serialize($this->preferences);
        Cookie::queue($this->cookieName, $serialized, $this->cookieLifetime);
    }

    private function validatePreferences($data)
    {
        $validated = [];
        
        foreach ($data as $key => $value) {
            // Validate key is string
            if (!is_string($key)) {
                continue;
            }
            
            // Validate value types (only scalar values and arrays)
            if (is_scalar($value) || is_array($value)) {
                $validated[$key] = $value;
            }
        }
        
        return $validated;
    }

    public function has($key)
    {
        return array_key_exists($key, $this->preferences);
    }

    public function count()
    {
        return count($this->preferences);
    }
}
